title: Saudi Quantum Research Access Program
entry_mechanism: KAUST-SDAIA joint programme with IBM Quantum and AWS Braket cloud access plus domestic quantum hardware procurement
executive_summary:
Quantum computing — using quantum mechanical phenomena (superposition, entanglement) to perform certain calculations exponentially faster than classical computers — is the technology likely to have the most transformative impact on materials science, drug discovery, optimization, and cryptography over the next 10–20 years. Saudi Arabia currently has zero domestic quantum computing capability — no quantum hardware, no quantum software engineers, and no quantum research programme of significance.

Node QT-4 targets Saudi Arabia's quantum computing access programme: cloud access to IBM Quantum and AWS Braket quantum systems for Saudi researchers, combined with the procurement of one small-scale domestic quantum computer (5–10 qubit trapped ion or superconducting system) as a research and training platform. The programme creates Saudi quantum computing researchers, quantum algorithm engineers, and the institutional knowledge base for Saudi Arabia to participate in the quantum computing era.

The global quantum computing market was valued at $1.3 billion in 2024 growing at 32.1% CAGR. Saudi Arabia's quantum research access programme is the necessary first step in a 10-year journey toward Saudi quantum computing capability — the alternative is importing quantum computing services as a permanent dependency.

companies.name: IBM Quantum
companies.rationale: IBM has the world's largest gate-based quantum computing programme and an established government access partnership model.

companies.name: IonQ
companies.rationale: IonQ produces the most accurate trapped-ion quantum computers and has government access programmes.

companies.name: Quantinuum (Honeywell/Cambridge Quantum)
companies.rationale: Quantinuum combines hardware and software with the most capable gate-based quantum computers available.

companies.name: AWS Braket
companies.rationale: AWS Braket provides cloud access to multiple quantum hardware platforms with Saudi Arabia AWS region deployment.

companies.name: Alice & Bob (France)
companies.rationale: Alice & Bob is building fault-tolerant quantum computers using cat qubits. French non-ITAR technology with government partnership model.

partners.name: KAUST Quantum Science
partners.current: Quantum physics research; some quantum algorithm research
partners.upgrade: KAUST IBM Quantum premium partnership; KAUST quantum algorithm research programme; 30 Saudi quantum computing researchers; first Saudi quantum computing publications

partners.name: SDAIA / National AI and Quantum
partners.current: National AI strategy; some quantum awareness
partners.upgrade: Saudi National Quantum Strategy implementation; quantum access programme funding; SDAIA quantum-AI convergence research programme

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Research
partners.current: HPC for reservoir simulation; some quantum chemistry awareness
partners.upgrade: Aramco quantum chemistry research for novel catalyst discovery; quantum optimization for supply chain; Aramco as commercial anchor for Saudi quantum research

partners.name: KACST Quantum Research
partners.current: Some quantum physics research; limited quantum computing
partners.upgrade: KACST domestic quantum computer procurement (IonQ or Quantinuum); Saudi quantum hardware research; quantum error correction research programme
